titleStmt.title: Letter from Rev. Thoms Gray to Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, 21 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas Gray

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Rev. Thomas Thompson Gray (1832-1924) to the registrar of the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) regarding an effort to repeal the School of Physic Act.
Rev. Thomas Thompson Gray served as the vice-provost of Trinity from 1916 until he resigned in 1924, a few weeks before his death.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.persName: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
REGISTRAR'S OFFICE,
TRINITY COLLEGE, DUBLIN. Febr. 21, 1916. Dear Sir, I am directed by the
Board of Trinity College to
enquire if the Royal College
of Physicians is willing to
act jointly with Trinity College
in an effort of obtain a repeal
of the School of Physic Act, on
the lines of the joint agreement
between the Board of Trinity and
the Fellows of the Royal College of
Physicians, laid before the recent
Royal Commission (on Trinity College
Dublin & on the University of Dublin),
and approved of, and recommended
by, that Commission. Will you kindly log this letter before
your board. Very Truly yours Thomas J. Gray,
Regr. Mr. Registrar
Royal College of Physicians. Macd.C. 5.III.16
